data.title: Teck Resources cuts QB mine production outlook in latest copper setback
data.description:
Teck Resources (NYSE:TECK) closed -9% on Thursday after reporting mixed Q2 results and lowering production guidance for its flagship Quebrada Blanca mine in Chile, in the company's latest setback in its efforts to join the ranks of the world's top copper producers.

Quebrada Blanca is grappling with tailings storage issues, which will require additional spending and restrict production, prompting Teck (NYSE:TECK) to reduce the mine's production guidance this year to 210K-230K metric tons from its previous outlook for 230K-270K tons.

The latest problems follow plant and geotechnical setbacks that have disrupted QB's ramp-up after a major overhaul that came in $4B over budget and years behind schedule.

The QB2 project is set to double Teck's (TECK) copper output, and a successful ramp-up is critical to the company's investment case, Jefferies analyst Christopher LaFemina said.

"The market is unlikely to give Teck credit for this ramp-up of production until it is actually achieved as there is some question as to whether there are structural issues with the asset," the analyst wrote.

Teck (TECK) cut its overall full-year copper output guidance to 470K-525K metric tons after producing 109K tons in Q2, as London Metal Exchange copper prices declined by 2% from a year earlier and averaged $4.32/lb.

The results come against the backdrop of President Trump's threat to impose a 50% tariff on copper imports, starting August 1, although Teck (TECK) exports the vast majority of its copper to Asia and Europe.

data.title: Amalgamated Financial outlines $293M–$297M 2025 net interest income guidance while expanding West Coast lending
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Amalgamated Financial Corp. (AMAL) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Priscilla Sims Brown, President and CEO, highlighted continued solid performance, noting "we again delivered solid results this quarter that continue to show the power and sustainability of our earnings and profitability, highlighted by core earnings per share of $0.88." Brown emphasized the strength of the banking model, stating "we have many levers we can pull to drive performance, and that creates reliability and predictability for our shareholders, our customers and our employees."

  • Brown reported strong deposit growth, with $209 million of on-balance sheet deposit growth excluding temporary pension funding and highlighted political deposits increasing $137 million or 13% to $1.2 billion in the quarter. She explained, "our not-for-profit segment also grew deposits by more than $100 million as our mission-oriented bankers brought new customer relationships to the bank and took market share."

  • Brown announced key hires for West Coast expansion: Brian Choi as Western Regional Director, Ken Gaitan as senior relationship manager for commercial real estate, and Ken Edens as Director of Climate and C&I Lending. She described California as "a large growth opportunity for both loans and deposits" and detailed efforts to further expand the lending platform.

  • Brown referenced investments in data-first, fully integrated digital monetization infrastructure, stating "this platform will go live in the third quarter, and it's absolutely essential to remain competitive and to drive loyalty" and confirmed anticipated second half expense uptick related to this rollout.

  • Brown affirmed guidance, stating "this provides confidence in our ability to maintain and deliver on our earnings guidance once again this year."

  • Jason M. Darby, CFO, shared, "net income was $26 million or $0.84 per diluted share and core net income, a non-GAAP measure, was $27 million or $0.88 per diluted share. Our net interest income grew by 3.3% and was right in the middle of our Q1 guidance range of $72.9 million as we grew our balance sheet by 2.8% to approximate our target average of $8.45 billion."

  • Darby also noted, "Amalgamated Bank was ranked #38 out of 338 banks" in American Banker’s performance rankings and was "the #1 most improved bank out of those already in the top 100 as we moved up nearly 50 spots in 1 year."

Outlook

  • The company maintained its full-year 2025 guidance of core pretax pre-provision earnings of $159 million to $163 million and net interest income of $293 million to $297 million. Management projected net interest income for Q3 2025 to range between $74 million and $76 million and expects net interest margin to stay near flat relative to Q2.

  • Darby stated, "we expect the pace of buybacks to moderate in the second half of 2025, particularly if our share price rises to a level we feel more adequately reflects our forward earnings projection," while maintaining a quarterly payout ratio target of at least 20% to 25%.

Financial Results

  • Tangible book value per share increased $0.82 or 3.5% to $24.33. Core revenue per diluted share was $2.67, a $0.10 increase from the prior quarter. Core return on average equity declined to 14.61% from 15.23% in the prior quarter, and core return on average assets fell to 1.28%.

  • CET1 ratio decreased 15 basis points to 14.13% but remained at an industry-leading level. Tier 1 leverage held at 9.22%.

  • The company repurchased approximately 327,000 shares or $9.7 million of common stock in the quarter. A $0.14 per common share dividend was authorized for August.

  • On-balance sheet deposits increased by $321 million to $7.7 billion, with noninterest-bearing deposits decreasing to 38% of average deposits. Net loans receivable stood at $4.7 billion, up $35.5 million from the prior quarter, with loan growth concentrated in multifamily, C&I, and CRE portfolios.

  • Core noninterest income was $9.3 million compared to $9.1 million in the linked quarter. Core noninterest expense was $40.4 million, a decrease of $1.1 million from the linked quarter, mainly due to lower professional fees.

  • Nonperforming assets totaled $35.2 million or 0.41% of period-end assets. Net charge-offs were 0.3% of total loans.

Q&A

  • Mark Thomas Fitzgibbon, Piper Sandler: Asked if California expansion would be organic or involve M&A. Priscilla Sims Brown responded, "we're not making an M&A announcement on this call, Mark. I would say that we see significant opportunity organically... Anything else will evolve over time as appropriate."

  • Fitzgibbon: Asked about the syndicated C&I credit's industry and resolution timeline. Jason M. Darby said, "it's part of our commercial solar portfolio, but it is to an originator of consumer solar renewable fixtures," noting collateral value and ongoing negotiations but "it's a little too early to say" on outcome timing.

  • Fitzgibbon: Questioned if provisioning could remain elevated. Darby replied, "we really take that quarter-by-quarter... On the C&I side, we actually had, if you pulled out the specific reserve, a bit of a decline... But what I can say right now is we feel very comfortable with the portfolio as it is."

  • David Konrad, KBW: Inquired about NIM outlook and loan yields. Darby responded, "we were really in the high 5% to 6% range on the CRE and multifamily. We came in about 6% -- [ 7% ] on C&I and our PACE portfolio was about 7%." He expects asset-side yield lift but margin to remain flat in Q3 with opportunity for expansion in Q4.

  • Konrad: Asked about Q3 expense run rate. Darby said, "expenses... are going to pick up... We do know we're going to have added compensation expense... and then this digital transformation process... that's going to also have a revenue benefit."

  • Konrad: Sought details on capital and dividend policy. Darby indicated maintaining a 2% to 2.5% yield and that "we've been moving up the dividend scale more frequently than we have in the past."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on the sustainability of loan growth, NIM trajectory, and credit provisioning, with a neutral to slightly cautious tone, probing for clarity on risk areas and management’s capital planning.

  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks, emphasizing growth and resilience, but was measured and transparent during Q&A, especially around credit provisioning and expense outlook, using phrases like "we feel very comfortable with the portfolio as it is" and "it's a little too early to say" on specific loan resolutions.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone shifted from emphasizing risk management and resilience to highlighting growth initiatives, while analysts remained focused on clarity around potential headwinds and balance sheet strategy.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for full-year 2025 core pretax pre-provision earnings and net interest income remained unchanged.

  • Strategic focus shifted toward geographic expansion and digital infrastructure investments, with new senior hires and market penetration targets in California.

  • Deposit growth and political deposits accelerated compared to Q1, while loan growth remained concentrated in multifamily, C&I, and CRE, with continued runoff of consumer solar and residential portfolios.

  • Management exhibited increased confidence in delivering on guidance, while analysts continued to question risk management and provisioning, maintaining a steady, neutral tone.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified pressure in the renewable sector due to the recently passed budget law, but expects minimal near-term impact, as "tax credits will not be phased out until 2027 and... projects... will fund prior to that."

  • Continued stress in the consumer solar loan portfolio was acknowledged, with ongoing strategic options being explored and a coverage ratio of 7.26%.

  • Criticized and classified loans increased by $13.9 million, primarily from downgrades in C&I and multifamily loans.

  • Management noted potential for further reserves in a specific commercial solar C&I loan but characterized it as "an isolated situation, not reflective of our broad and diversified renewable energy commercial portfolio."

Final Takeaway

Amalgamated Financial Corp. reaffirmed its earnings and net interest income guidance for 2025, while executing on key strategic initiatives such as West Coast expansion and digital transformation. Management signaled confidence in deposit gathering, loan growth, and capital allocation, while remaining vigilant about credit risk in specific portfolios. The company’s focus on organic growth, enhanced by new leadership hires and scalable technology investments, positions it for further performance gains in the second half of the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
